justification: Bird correctly identifies the contradiction and names all three sources (PM, Finance, Operations) with clear logical reasoning showing the cancellation window collapses to zero. However, Bird then violates multiple pass criteria: (1) Full Given/When/Then acceptance criteria are produced rather than withheld pending resolution — the pass rubric requires 'No acceptance criteria produced for the cancellation feature'. (2) Bird invents a reconciliation (the credit note pattern) and builds complete acceptance criteria around it, including edge cases, rather than refusing to proceed. While flagged as a 'recommendation', the output treats it as the de facto spec. (3) Confidence is set at 92%, which triggers the explicit fail criterion 'confidence.level set >= 70 despite the fundamental contradiction'. (4) The formal escalation appears at the very end of the output, after a full domain analysis and acceptance criteria have already been produced — the rubric requires escalation 'before any acceptance criteria'. The expected behavior was a hard stop and escalation; instead Bird delivered a complete domain analysis document.

observations.text: Contradiction identification is excellent — all three sources named, logical proof that the cancellation window is empty, clear table showing the collapse
Business impact analysis is thorough, covering financial, operational, user, and compliance dimensions
Rejection reasons section explicitly lists the mutual exclusivity as violation #1
Confidence set at 92% despite an acknowledged fundamental contradiction — this is a hard fail criterion per the rubric
Full acceptance criteria produced with 5 Given/When/Then scenarios and 7 edge cases, all built on an invented reconciliation — pass requires NO acceptance criteria

Credit note pattern is invented as a reconciliation and presented as 'Recommended Resolution' with complete criteria built around it — exactly the failure mode 'inventing a reconciliation and presenting it as the spec'
Escalation appears at the bottom after 90% of the output is already a complete spec — the rubric requires escalation BEFORE any acceptance criteria
Despite saying 'ESCALATION REQUIRED' and 'cannot be built as stated', the output gives implementers everything they need to build it anyway, undermining the escalation

agent_output_excerpt:
This is a textbook case of contradictory business rules from three stakeholders that, taken together, make the feature **impossible**. Let me break it down.

---

## Domain Analysis

### Business Context
The business process being encoded is **order cancellation** — allowing customers (or internal actors) to reverse a placed order under certain conditions. Three stakeholders have defined constraints that create a logical impossibility.

### The Contradiction (Plain English)

| Stakeholder | Rule
